repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Drop the bundled busted and luacheck test dependency path in favor of a
repo-local Lua harness.

close https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/32536
ref https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/34592

comment_data.body: replacing busted with ~2 files is extremely nice and lets us make progress towards simplifying busted, dropping its "magic", and being able to hook into the lifecycle.

Sneak yeet of `luacheck` ;) (I approve!)

Previously discussed at #32536

Good we don't need this cowboy programmed hack anymore

@@ -1,91 +0,0 @@
-local platform = vim.uv.os_uname()
-local deps_install_dir = table.remove(_G.arg, 1)
-_G.c_include_path = {}
-while vim.startswith(_G.arg[1], '-I') do
-  table.insert(_G.c_include_path, string.sub(table.remove(_G.arg, 1), 3))
-end
-local subcommand = table.remove(_G.arg, 1)
-local suffix = (platform and platform.sysname:lower():find 'windows') and '.dll' or '.so'
-package.path = (deps_install_dir .. '/?.lua;')
-  .. (deps_install_dir .. '/?/init.lua;')
-  .. package.path
-package.cpath = deps_install_dir .. '/?' .. suffix .. ';' .. package.cpath
-
-local uv = vim.uv
-
--- we use busted and luacheck and their lua dependencies
--- But installing their binary dependencies with luarocks is very
--- slow, replace them with vim.uv wrappers

+--- Return the current wall-clock time in seconds.

+  local github_actions = ((any_provider or name == 'github') and nil ~= os.getenv('GITHUB_ACTIONS'))
+  local cirrus_ci = ((any_provider or name == 'cirrus') and nil ~= os.getenv('CIRRUS_CI'))
+  return github_actions or cirrus_ci
+end
+
+--- Read the last `keep` lines from a file.
+--- @param path string
+--- @param keep integer
+--- @return string[]?
+local function read_tail_lines(path, keep)
+  local file = io.open(path, 'r')
+  if not file then
+    return nil
+  end
+
+  local lines = {}
+  for line in file:lines() do
+    lines[#lines + 1] = line
+    if #lines > keep then
+      table.remove(lines, 1)
+    end
+  end
+  file:close()
+  return lines
+end
+

+--- Read and optionally rename the current Nvim log for failure output.
+--- @param logfile? string
+--- @param ci_rename? boolean

+  logfile = logfile or os.getenv('NVIM_LOG_FILE') or 'nvim.log'
+  if not uv.fs_stat(logfile) then
+    return nil
+  end
+
+  local ci = M.is_ci()
+  local keep = ci and 100 or 10
+  local lines = read_tail_lines(logfile, keep) or {}
+  local separator = ('-'):rep(78)
+  local parts = {
+    separator,
+    '\n',
+    string.format('$NVIM_LOG_FILE: %s\n', logfile),
+    #lines > 0 and string.format('(last %d lines)\n', keep) or '(empty)\n',
+  }
+  for _, line in ipairs(lines) do
+    parts[#parts + 1] = line
+    parts[#parts + 1] = '\n'
+  end
+  if ci and ci_rename then
+    os.rename(logfile, logfile .. '.displayed')
+  end
+  parts[#parts + 1] = separator
+  parts[#parts + 1] = '\n'
+  return table.concat(parts)
+end

+--- @param path string
+--- @return boolean
+local function is_absolute_path(path)
+  return path:sub(1, 1) == '/' or path:match('^%a:[/\\]') ~= nil or path:match('^[/\\][/\\]') ~= nil

+--- @class test.luassert
+local M = {}
+
+local FORMAT_DEPTH = 100
+
+--- @param value any
+--- @return string
+local function format_value(value)
+  if type(value) == 'string' then
+    return string.format('%q', value)
+  end
+
+  local ok, inspected = pcall(vim.inspect, value, { depth = FORMAT_DEPTH })
+  if ok then
+    return inspected
+  end
+
+  return tostring(value)
+end
+
+--- @param condition boolean
+--- @param value any
+--- @param context any
+--- @param fallback string
+local function assert_value(condition, value, context, fallback)
+  if not condition then
+    local message = context ~= nil and tostring(context) or fallback
+    error(message, 3)
+  end
+  return value
+end
+
+--- @param expected any
+--- @param actual any
+--- @param comparator string
+--- @return string
+local function comparison_message(expected, actual, comparator)
+  return ('Expected values to be %s.\nExpected:\n%s\nActual:\n%s'):format(
+    comparator,
+    format_value(expected),
+    format_value(actual)
+  )
+end
+
+--- @param expected any
+--- @param actual any
+--- @return boolean
+local function equal(expected, actual)
+  return expected == actual or vim.deep_equal(expected, actual)

+
+--- @param predicate fun(expected: any, actual: any): boolean
+--- @param expected any
+--- @param actual any
+--- @param context any
+--- @param comparator string
+--- @return any
+local function assert_comparison(predicate, expected, actual, context, comparator)
+  return assert_value(

+    actual,

+    comparison_message(expected, actual, comparator)
+  )

+
+--- @param expected any

+--- @param context? any
+--- @return any
+function M.eq(expected, actual, context)
+  return assert_comparison(equal, expected, actual, context, 'equal')
+end

comment_data.body:
The test harness needs to do some isolation of tests, otherwise they can leak into each other.

For nvim this is less needed as all the tests run through an embedded instance which gives us greater isolation, but I still think some basic snapshotting and restoration of the main lua globals is valuable.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -37,12 +37,25 @@ You can learn [Lua concepts 15 minutes](https://learnxinyminutes.com/docs/lua/),
 see also |lua-guide|. Use any existing test as a template to start writing new
 tests, or see |dev-quickstart|.
 
-Tests are run by the `/cmake/RunTests.cmake` script using `busted` (a Lua test-runner).
+Tests are run by the `/cmake/RunTests.cmake` script using the repo-local Lua
+test harness in `/test/harness.lua`.
 For some failures, `./build/nvim.log` (or `$NVIM_LOG_FILE`) may provide insight.
 
 Depending on the presence of binaries (e.g., `xclip`) some tests will be
 skipped.
 
+Harness isolation                                  *dev-test-harness-isolation*
+
+The Lua harness runs all selected spec files in one low-level `nvim -ll`
+process, then restores a baseline before each file. For each suite iteration,
+the file boundary baseline is captured after `--helper` is loaded, so
+helper-provided modules and defaults persist across files.
+Helper files are preload-only: they may require modules, set defaults, and
+register suite-end cleanup, but they do not define tests or hooks.
+
+The harness restores `_G`, `package.loaded`, `package.preload`, `arg`, and the
+process environment with the same shallow file boundary model that busted uses.

comment_data.body:
I've only been using `make functionaltest` (as I always have).

I see now that we now have extra special code in `test.yml`

```bash
set +e
# `-k 0` tells ninja to continue running all targets even if some fail.
cmake --build build --target functionaltest-parallel -j 2 -- -k 0
exit_code="$?"
cmake --build build --target functionaltest-summary
exit "$exit_code"
```

And the behaviour you are seeing only comes from `functionaltest-summary` which I didn't test.

This code really should be in the Makefile.

comment_regular_data.body: Not a blocker, and not required for the MVP, but it would be extremely nice to expose this for plugins (which I assume should "just work", in which case it just needs a minimal documentation). I really don't want to keep https://github.com/nvim-treesitter/plentest.nvim around...
I want to expose something for plugins, but not everything. E.g. `screen.lua` needs some work before public exposure.
Yeah, plugins don't need the full infrastructure, just a lightweight harness that can replace busted/plenary.test_harness/plentest. It's also clearly not the primary driver for this change.
Looking at the code, it seems not clear what the distinction between a failure and an error is any more. Previously using `error()` in a test leads to an error, while now it leads to a failure.

I removed that distinction as I didn't see what value it added?

Can you explain to me why you would want to `fail` instead of `error`? Aren't they practically the same?
I missed changes in the reporter. Will remove the failure parts.
I don't understand what you are doing. I tested your PR and even explicit `error()` calls are reported as failures, so why are you trying to remove the failure reporting and keep the error reporting?

I added error modes back, but they can only be triggered outside of tests (`before_each` etc). `error()` in a test (`it()`) is still classed as a failure.

You seem to have strong opinions about this, so can you explain how you would want to model this? The main thing I wanted to simplify was calling `luassert.fail()` and `luassert.epicfail()` in some tests, which could just be replaced with `error()`. That allowed me to make `luassert.lua` stateless.

I'm mainly talking about the reporting of failures. The current behavior here doesn't look right:
```lua
function Reporter:summary_footer_noun(status, count)
  if status == 'error' then
    return count == 1 and 'ERROR' or 'ERRORS'
  end

  return count == 1 and 'TEST' or 'TESTS'
end
```
If there are 2 skipped tests and 1 test failure, at the end it shows
```
2 TESTS
1 TEST
```
which is very confusing.

Full summary I got in a run:
```
======== 200 tests from 1 test file of test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua ran. (20020.24 ms total)
PASSED   197 tests.
SKIPPED  2 tests, listed below:
SKIPPED  ...ovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua @ 1779: LSP basic_init test should check the body and didChange incremental normal mode editing
SKIPPED  ...ovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua @ 6010: LSP vim.lsp._watchfiles sends notifications when files change (watchfunc=inotify)
inotify-tools not installed and not on CI
FAILED   1 test, listed below:
FAILED   ...ovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua @ 5502: LSP cmd connects to lsp server via rpc.connect using hostname
...ovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua:5502: Expected values to be equal.
Expected:
true
Actual:
false

stack traceback:
	[C]: in function 'error'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/luassert.lua:28: in function 'eq'
	...ovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua:76: in function 'verify_single_notification'
	...ovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua:5502: in function <...ovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua:5495>
	[C]: in function 'xpcall'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:671: in function 'run_callable'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:984: in function 'run_test'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:1059: in function 'run_suite'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:1057: in function 'run_suite'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:1057: in function 'run_suite'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:1057: in function 'run_suite'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:1486: in function 'run_test_file'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:1556: in function 'run_iteration'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:1639: in function 'main'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/runner.lua:30: in main chunk

 2 TESTS
 1 TEST
```

The test IDs also don't look right:
```
-------- Global test environment setup.
-------- Running tests from test/functional/api/autocmd_spec.lua
RUN       autocmd api nvim_create_autocmd validation: 20.31 ms OK
RUN      T1x autocmd api nvim_create_autocmd doesnt leak when you use ++once: 19.22 ms OK
RUN      T2x autocmd api nvim_create_autocmd allows passing buffer by key: 19.39 ms OK
RUN      T3x autocmd api nvim_create_autocmd does not allow passing invalid buffers: 17.70 ms OK
```
On this PR, the first test has no test ID, and all remain tests have an `x` suffix. Previously the `x` suffix is mainly used for skipped tests:
```lua
        -- Use a different test ID for skipped tests as well as Nvim instances spawned
        -- between this after_each() and the next before_each() (e.g. in setup()).
        _G._nvim_test_id = _G._nvim_test_id .. 'x'
```

> (And potentially, extracting to a nvim-test repo, though that may not be necessary/desired; the "isolation" is the main goal.)

That is not needed if we bundle and expose `vim.test` (placeholder name, need not be in the `vim` namespace); the standalone repo would only be needed if we didn't (so Nvim and plugins can both pull the deps for `make test`).

Let's not race ahead on exposing anything. The main goal of this change is to remove legacy dependencies, which will unlock more cleanup tasks.

There's still a lot more to be done before we can think about exposing anything. There are still a lot of scattered modules and no real API shape, nor is there any documentation.
(The same applies for "exporting" it; my point was not to push for either _now_.)
